2605.04875.title: Anticipating Innovation Using Large Language Models
2605.04875.paper_summary: Forecasting innovation, intended as the emergence of new technological combinations, is a fundamental challenge for science and policy. We show that forthcoming combinations leave an early trace in the collective language of patents, with predictive signals detectable even decades in advance. We show that signal is not attributable to any single inventor, but emerges as a collective shift in how technologies are described across thousands of patents. To this end, we introduce TechToken, a transformer-based model that treats technologies, classified by International Patent Classification codes, as words in its vocabulary, learning the language of technologies by embedding these codes during fine-tuning. We define context similarity between code embeddings as a measure of linguistic convergence and show that it accurately predicts first technological combinations. TechToken also improves general representation quality, outperforming state-of-the-art models across different patent-related tasks.
2605.04875.paper_summary_zh: 預測創新，即新技術組合的出現，是科學和政策面臨的一項根本挑戰。我們展示即將出現的組合在專利的集體語言中留下早期痕跡，預測信號甚至可以提前幾十年檢測到。我們顯示這一信號並非歸因於任何單一發明者，而是作為一種集體轉變出現，體現在數千項專利中技術描述的變化。為此，我們引入了 TechToken，這是一種基於Transformer的模型，將按國際專利分類代碼分類的技術視為其詞彙中的單詞，通過在微調過程中嵌入這些代碼來學習技術的語言。我們將代碼嵌入之間的上下文相似性定義為語言收斂的度量，並顯示它準確預測了首次技術組合。TechToken 還提高了通用表示質量，在不同的專利相關任務中超越了最先進的模型。
2605.04875.author: Enrico Maria Fenoaltea et.al.
2605.04875.authors: Enrico Maria Fenoaltea, Filippo Santoro, Giordano De Marzo, Segun Taofeek Aroyehun, Andrea Tacchella
